items.title: Gradients
items.description: ∇L(θ) supplies the direction and magnitude for each update; gradient descent consumes the gradient vector computed via partial derivatives and autograd.

items.title: Loss surfaces
items.description: Parameters θ are coordinates on a loss surface; each GD step moves θ downhill locally, and surface shape (valleys, saddles, plateaus) governs convergence behavior.

items.title: Learning rate
items.description: η in θ ← θ − η∇L(θ) controls step length; too large causes divergence or oscillation, too small slows progress—tuning η is separate from the update rule itself.

items.title: Stochastic gradient descent
items.description: SGD uses the same update rule θ ← θ − η∇L but estimates ∇L from one or a few examples per step instead of the full batch, trading exactness for speed and noise.
